main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_session_state_t
Client connection session state for session_client_like integration.

Functions


int client_main (void)
Client mode entry point for unified binary.

Variables


thread_pool_t * g_client_worker_pool = NULL
Global client worker thread pool. app_client_t * g_client = NULL
Global client application context. struct webrtc_peer_manager * g_peer_manager = NULL
Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility).

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

This module serves as the main entry point for the ascii-chat client application. It orchestrates the entire client lifecycle including initialization, connection management, and the primary event loop that manages reconnection logic.

Architecture Overview

The client follows a modular threading architecture:

  • Main thread: Connection management and event coordination
  • Data reception thread: Handles incoming packets from server
  • Ping thread: Maintains connection keepalive
  • Webcam capture thread: Captures and transmits video frames
  • Audio capture thread: Captures and transmits audio data (optional)

Connection Management

The client implements robust reconnection logic with exponential backoff:

1.
Initial connection attempt
2.
On connection loss, attempt reconnection with increasing delays
3.
Maximum delay cap to prevent excessive wait times
4.
Clean thread lifecycle management across reconnections

Thread Lifecycle

Each connection cycle follows this pattern:

1.
Connection Establishment: Socket creation and server handshake
2.
Thread Spawning: Start all worker threads for the connection
3.
Active Monitoring: Monitor connection health and thread status
4.
Connection Loss Detection: Detect broken connections via thread exit
5.
Cleanup Phase: Join all threads and reset connection state
6.
Reconnection Cycle: Repeat from step 1 unless shutdown requested

Integration Points

  • server.c: Connection establishment and socket management
  • protocol.c: Initial capability negotiation and join packets
  • display.c: Terminal initialization and control
  • capture.c: Media capture subsystem initialization
  • audio.c: Audio subsystem initialization (if enabled)
  • keepalive.c: Ping thread management

Error Handling

The main loop implements graceful error recovery:

  • Fatal initialization errors cause immediate exit
  • Network errors trigger reconnection attempts
  • Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGINT, SIGWINCH)
  • Resource cleanup on all exit paths

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket operations and network initialization
  • Thread management and synchronization
  • Signal handling differences between Unix and Windows
  • Terminal I/O and control sequences

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

Definition in file client/main.c.

Function Documentation

int client_main (void )

Client mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete client lifecycle including:

  • Client-specific subsystem initialization (display, capture, audio)
  • Server connection establishment with exponential backoff reconnection
  • Main event loop coordinating connection state and thread lifecycle
  • Media streaming (webcam frames and optional audio)
  • Frame display to terminal
  • Graceful shutdown and resource cleanup

Lifecycle Overview

The client follows this high-level state flow:

1.
Initialize: Set up display, capture, audio subsystems
2.
Connect Loop:
•
Establish TCP connection to server
•
Perform cryptographic handshake
•
Spawn worker threads (data reception, capture, keepalive, audio)
•
Monitor connection health
•
Detect connection loss via thread exit or socket errors
3.
Cleanup: Stop threads, close socket, reset terminal
4.
Reconnect: Exponential backoff, retry (unless fatal auth error)
5.
Exit: On user signal (SIGINT) or fatal error

Connection Failure Handling

Transient Errors (retryable):

  • Connection refused, timeout, DNS failure → exponential backoff retry
  • Network temporarily unavailable → wait and retry

Fatal Errors (no retry):

  • Authentication failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED) → exit immediately
  • Host key verification failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED) → exit immediately

Thread Management

The client spawns several worker threads coordinated by the main thread:

  • Data Reception Thread: Receives and processes server packets
  • Webcam Capture Thread: Captures frames, transmits to server
  • Ping/Keepalive Thread: Sends periodic pings, detects timeouts
  • Audio Threads (optional): Captures/plays audio with Opus encoding

All threads check the global g_should_exit flag and exit cleanly on shutdown. Main thread waits for all threads to exit before reconnection or final cleanup.

Options

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher (src/main.c) before this function is called. They are available via the global options API (GET_OPTION, etc.).

Key options affecting behavior:

  • opt_address: Server address or session string
  • opt_port: Server port (default 27224)
  • opt_audio_enabled: Enable audio capture/playback
  • opt_snapshot: Capture single frame and exit
  • opt_client_key, opt_server_key: SSH keys for authentication

Returns

Exit code:
  • 0: Success (clean exit or snapshot completed)
  • 1: Initialization/configuration error
  • 2: Connection fatal error (auth failed)
  • 130: Signal interrupt (SIGINT/Ctrl+C)

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat client --address localhost --audio
# Dispatcher (src/main.c) handles:
# - Option parsing
# - Log initialization
# - Signal handler setup
# Then calls client_main() which handles:
# - Subsystem init (display, capture, audio)
# - Connection loop with reconnection logic
# - Thread coordination
# - Graceful shutdown

See also

topic_client "Client Architecture Overview"

topic_client_main "Client Lifecycle Details"

topic_client_connection "Connection Management"

topic_client_protocol "Packet Processing"

Definition at line 628 of file client/main.c.

628                       {
629   log_debug("client_main() starting");
630 
631   // Register client crypto callbacks for TCP connection path
632   // The TCP path in lib/network/connection_attempt.c relies on these callbacks
633   // to handle cryptographic operations and key exchange
634   static const app_callbacks_t client_crypto_callbacks = {
635       .should_exit = should_exit,
636       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
637       .platform_get_monotonic_time_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us,
638       .platform_sleep_ms = platform_sleep_ms,
639       .server_connection_set_ip = server_connection_set_ip,
640       .server_connection_set_port = server_connection_set_port,
641       .client_crypto_set_mode = client_crypto_set_mode,
642       .client_crypto_init = client_crypto_init,
643       .client_crypto_handshake = client_crypto_handshake,
644       .crypto_client_is_ready = crypto_client_is_ready,
645       .crypto_client_get_context = crypto_client_get_context,
646   };
647   app_callbacks_register(&client_crypto_callbacks);
648 
649   // Initialize client-specific systems (NOT shared with session_client_like)
650   // This includes: thread pool, display layer, app client context, server connection
651   int init_result = initialize_client_systems();
652   if (init_result != 0) {
653 #ifndef NDEBUG
654     // Debug builds: automatically fall back to test pattern if webcam is in use
655     if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE && !GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
656       log_warn("Webcam is in use - automatically falling back to test pattern mode (debug build only)");
657 
658       // Enable test pattern mode via RCU update
659       asciichat_error_t update_result = options_set_bool("test_pattern", true);
660       if (update_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
661         log_error("Failed to update options for test pattern fallback");
662         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
663       }
664 
665       // Retry initialization with test pattern enabled
666       init_result = initialize_client_systems();
667       if (init_result != 0) {
668         log_error("Failed to initialize even with test pattern fallback");
669         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
670         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
671       }
672       log_debug("Successfully initialized with test pattern fallback");
673 
674       // Clear the error state since we successfully recovered
675       CLEAR_ERRNO();
676     } else
677 #endif
678     {
679       // Release builds or other errors: print help and exit
680       if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION) {
681         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
682         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
683       }
684       // For other errors, just exit with the error code
685       return init_result;
686     }
687   }
688 
689   // Register cleanup function for graceful shutdown
690   (void)atexit(shutdown_client);
691 
692   // Register client interrupt callback (socket shutdown on SIGTERM/Ctrl+C)
693   // Global signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGPIPE, Ctrl+C) are set up in setup_signal_handlers() in src/main.c
694   set_interrupt_callback(server_connection_shutdown);
695 
696 #ifndef _WIN32
697   // Register SIGWINCH for terminal resize handling (client-specific, not in framework)
698   platform_signal(SIGWINCH, client_handle_sigwinch);
699 #endif
700 
701   /* ========================================================================
702    * Client-Specific: LAN/Session Discovery
703    * ========================================================================
704    * This phase discovers the server to connect to via:
705    * - LAN discovery (mDNS)
706    * - Session string lookup (ACDS)
707    * - Direct address/port
708    */
709 
710   // LAN Discovery: If --scan flag is set, discover servers on local network
711   const options_t *opts = options_get();
712   if (opts && opts->lan_discovery &&
713       (opts->address[0] == '\0' || is_localhost_ipv4(opts->address) || strcmp(opts->address, "localhost") == 0)) {
714     log_debug("LAN discovery: --scan flag set, querying for available servers");
715 
716     ui_mdns_config_t lan_config;
717     memset(&lan_config, 0, sizeof(lan_config));
718     lan_config.timeout_ms = 2 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Wait up to 2 seconds for responses
719     lan_config.max_servers = 20;                // Support up to 20 servers on LAN
720     lan_config.quiet = true;                    // Quiet during discovery, TUI will show status
721 
722     int discovered_count = 0;
723     ui_mdns_server_t *discovered_servers = ui_mdns_query(&lan_config, &discovered_count);
724 
725     // Use TUI for server selection
726     int selected_index = ui_mdns_select(discovered_servers, discovered_count);
727 
728     if (selected_index < 0) {
729       // User cancelled or no servers found
730       if (discovered_count == 0) {
731         // No servers found - log message and prevent any further output
732         // Lock the terminal so other threads can't write and our error
733         // will be the last message
734         log_lock_terminal();
735 
736         // Log single message with embedded newlines to prevent multiple log entries
737         log_error("No ascii-chat servers found on the local network.\nUse 'ascii-chat client <address>' to connect "
738                   "manually.");
739 
740         // Exit without cleanup
741         platform_force_exit(1);
742       }
743       // User cancelled (had servers to choose from but pressed cancel)
744       log_debug("LAN discovery: User cancelled server selection");
745       if (discovered_servers) {
746         ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
747       }
748       return 1; // User cancelled
749     }
750 
751     // Update options with discovered server's address and port
752     ui_mdns_server_t *selected = &discovered_servers[selected_index];
753     const char *selected_address = ui_mdns_get_best_address(selected);
754 
755     // We need to modify options, but they're immutable via RCU
756     // Create a new options copy with updated address/port
757     options_t *opts_new = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(options_t), options_t *);
758     if (opts_new) {
759       memcpy(opts_new, opts, sizeof(options_t));
760       SAFE_STRNCPY(opts_new->address, selected_address, sizeof(opts_new->address));
761 
762       opts_new->port = (int)selected->port;
763 
764       log_debug("LAN discovery: Selected server '%s' at %s:%d", selected->name, opts_new->address, opts_new->port);
765 
766       // Note: In a real scenario, we'd update the global options via RCU
767       // For now, we'll use the updated values directly for connection
768       // This is a known limitation - proper RCU update requires more infrastructure
769     }
770 
771     ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
772   }
773 
774   // =========================================================================
775   // Client-Specific: Server Address Resolution
776   // =========================================================================
777   // Client mode supports:
778   // 1. Direct address/port (--address HOST --port PORT) - handled by options
779   // 2. LAN discovery (--scan) - handled above
780   // 3. WebSocket URL (direct ws:// or wss:// connection string)
781   //
782   // Note: Session string discovery via ACDS is handled by discovery mode only.
783   //       Client mode does NOT use ACDS or session strings.
784 
785   const char *discovered_address = NULL;
786   int discovered_port = 0;
787 
788   // Check if user provided a WebSocket URL as the server address
789   const options_t *opts_websocket = options_get();
790   const char *provided_address = opts_websocket && opts_websocket->address[0] != '\0' ? opts_websocket->address : NULL;
791 
792   if (provided_address && url_is_websocket(provided_address)) {
793     // Direct WebSocket connection
794     log_debug("Client: Direct WebSocket URL: %s", provided_address);
795     discovered_address = provided_address;
796     discovered_port = 0; // Port is embedded in the URL
797   }
798 
799   if (discovered_port > 0) {
800     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s, discovered_port=%d", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL",
801               discovered_port);
802   } else {
803     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL");
804   }
805 
806   // Store discovered address/port in session state for client_run() callback
807   static char address_storage[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
808   if (discovered_address) {
809     SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, discovered_address, sizeof(address_storage));
810     g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
811     g_client_session.discovered_port = discovered_port;
812   } else {
813     const options_t *opts_fallback = options_get();
814     if (opts_fallback && opts_fallback->address[0] != '\0') {
815       SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, opts_fallback->address, sizeof(address_storage));
816       g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
817       g_client_session.discovered_port = opts_fallback->port;
818     } else {
819       g_client_session.discovered_address = "localhost";
820       g_client_session.discovered_port = 27224;
821     }
822   }
823 
824   // Initialize connection context for first attempt
825   asciichat_error_t ctx_init_result = connection_context_init(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
826   if (ctx_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
827     log_error("Failed to initialize connection context");
828     return 1;
829   }
830 
831   /* ========================================================================
832    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
833    * ========================================================================
834    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
835    * - Terminal output management (force stderr if piped)
836    * - Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
837    * - Splash screen lifecycle
838    * - Media source selection (webcam, file, URL, test pattern)
839    * - FPS probing for media files
840    * - Audio initialization and lifecycle
841    * - Display context creation
842    * - Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)
843    *
844    * Client mode provides:
845    * - client_run() callback: connection loop, protocol startup, monitoring
846    * - client_should_reconnect() callback: reconnection policy
847    * - Reconnection configuration: attempts, delay, callbacks
848    */
849 
850   // Get reconnect attempts setting (-1 = unlimited, 0 = no retry, >0 = retry N times)
851   int reconnect_attempts = GET_OPTION(reconnect_attempts);
852 
853   // Configure session_client_like with client-specific settings
854   session_client_like_config_t config = {
855       .run_fn = client_run,
856       .run_user_data = NULL,
857       .kind = SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
858       .discovery = NULL,
859       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
860       .exit_user_data = NULL,
861       .keyboard_handler = NULL, // Client mode: server drives display
862       .max_reconnect_attempts = reconnect_attempts,
863       .should_reconnect_callback = client_should_reconnect,
864       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
865       .reconnect_delay_ms = 1000,         // 1 second delay between reconnection attempts
866       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/client manages cursor
867   };
868 
869   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call session_client_like_run() with %d attempts", reconnect_attempts);
870   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
871   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
872 
873   // Network client lifecycle is owned by connection_context_cleanup() after the session returns.
874 
875   // Cleanup connection context
876   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call connection_context_cleanup()");
877   connection_context_cleanup(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
878   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] connection_context_cleanup() returned");
879 
880   // Cleanup session log buffer (used by splash screen in session_client_like)
881   // NOTE: session_log_buffer_destroy() is already called in session_client_like_run()
882   // during its cleanup phase, so we don't call it here to avoid double-destroy of mutex
883 
884   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] ascii-chat client shutting down");
885 
886   // IMPORTANT: Stop worker threads and join them BEFORE memory report
887   // atexit(shutdown_client) won't run if interrupted by SIGTERM, so call explicitly
888   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call shutdown_client()");
889   fflush(stderr);
890   shutdown_client();
891   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] shutdown_client() returned");
892   fflush(stderr);
893 
894   // Cleanup remaining shared subsystems (buffer pool, platform, etc.)
895   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call asciichat_shared_destroy()");
896   asciichat_shared_destroy();
897 
898   // Log final session statistics before exit
899   // Note: Frame count is logged in protocol_stop_connection() if connection was established
900   // This is a fallback in case the connection never started
901   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : 1;
902 }




References options_state::address,
    app_callbacks_register(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL,
    CLEAR_ERRNO, client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_crypto_init(), client_crypto_set_mode(),
    connection_context_cleanup(), connection_context_init(),
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    ERROR_WEBCAM, ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE,
    ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION, FATAL, GET_OPTION,
    is_localhost_ipv4(), options_state::lan_discovery,
    log_debug, log_error, log_lock_terminal(),
    log_warn, ui_mdns_config_t::max_servers,
    MS_PER_SEC_INT, ui_mdns_server_t::name, options_get(),
    options_set_bool(), platform_force_exit(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_signal(),
    platform_sleep_ms(), options_state::port,
    ui_mdns_server_t::port, ui_mdns_config_t::quiet,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY, server_connection_set_ip(),
    server_connection_set_port(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT, session_client_like_run(),
    set_interrupt_callback(), should_exit(), signal_exit(),
    ui_mdns_config_t::timeout_ms, ui_mdns_free_results(),
    ui_mdns_get_best_address(), ui_mdns_query(),
    ui_mdns_select(), url_is_websocket(), and
    webcam_print_init_error_help().








Variable
  Documentation




app_client_t* g_client = NULL


Global client application context. Global application client
    context




Central connection and application state for the client. Contains
    transport-agnostic state: audio, threads, display, crypto. Network-specific
    state (socket, connection flags) is in the active transport client.




Initialized by app_client_create() in client_main().
    Destroyed by app_client_destroy() at cleanup.


Definition at line 152 of file client/main.c.






thread_pool_t* g_client_worker_pool = NULL


Global client worker thread pool. Global client worker thread
  pool




Manages all client worker threads including:


    
  
  • Data reception thread (protocol.c)
    • 
  
  • Webcam capture thread (capture.c)
    • 
  
  • Ping/keepalive thread (keepalive.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio capture thread (audio.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio sender thread (audio.c)
    •

    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    keepalive_start_thread(), protocol_start_connection(), and
    protocol_stop_connection().






struct
  webrtc_peer_manager* g_peer_manager = NULL


Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility). Global WebRTC
    peer manager for P2P connections.




Client mode no longer uses WebRTC, but protocol.c still references
    this. Always NULL in client mode.


Definition at line 160 of file client/main.c.
